title: rubus discolor
intro: Rubus discolor, also known as the New Zealand blackberry, is a species of flowering plant in the rose family that is native to New Zealand and Australia. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The plant's leaves and roots are rich in nutrients and have been used to treat various health conditions, such as digestive issues and skin problems, due to its health benefits. Rubus discolor can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or salve for herbal use, and it is also used in some commercial skincare products.
